No sacrifices! With over 30-feet of Park Avenue frontage and exposures in all four cardinal directions, this sun-filled 9-room apartment is located in 580 Park Avenue, a 1923-built Neo-Renaissance masterpiece by architectural luminary James E.R. Carpenter. 

Accessed via a semi-private elevator landing, this supremely elegant apartment affords picturesque tree-lined views and oodles of prewar charm! It is anchored by an elegant circular gallery, one of Carpenter's signatures, which leads to a truly impressive suite of entertaining rooms including a grand living room with wood burning fireplace and stunning original ceiling detail, a formal dining room, and a den plus adjacent home office that were previously two staff’s quarters.

The home's eat-in windowed kitchen is a chef's dream, boasting a walk-in pantry and top-of-the-line appliances including 6-burner Viking Professional range, Viking Professional range hood, Sub-Zero refrigerator, Sub-Zero wine cooler, dishwasher, ice maker, and washer/dryer.

The home is further enhanced by stunning herringbone floors in immaculate repair, three full bathrooms, 9.5-foot ceilings, amazing closet space including multiple walk-ins, and a 6’ x 12’ private storage unit!

580 Park Avenue is one of the finest pre-war apartment houses in New York. Sheathed in light-tan brick, the building features a four-story limestone base with a stylized colonnade formed by a series of pilasters, a magnificent entrance flanked by fluted pilasters and classical stone-cut planters, and an exquisite lobby inspired by Italian Renaissance palazzos. 

Residents of 580 Park Avenue enjoy 24-hour doorman, elevator attendants, and a live-in resident manager. Permitted: pets, pied-a-terre, and financing up to $1M. 3% flip tax paid by buyer.
BROWN HARRIS STEVENS RESIDENTIAL SALES LLC
445 Park Avenue 11th Fl, New York, NY 10022

100 feet of Central Park frontage. 

Introducing Residence 15A, a stunning fusion of modern design and timeless elegance in a premier Fifth Avenue condo building. This fully renovated, impeccably curated home redefines luxury living. With 11 expansive windows spanning 100 feet of direct views over Central Park and Fifth Avenue, the vistas are nothing short of spectacular, capturing the essence of New York City from every angle.

This sophisticated residence currently features 3 bedrooms, easily adaptable to 4-bedrooms, with 4.5 opulent bathrooms. The interiors are graced with brand-new herringbone hardwood floors, custom stone tiles in the kitchen, and Waterworks-designed bathrooms, all seamlessly integrated with advanced smart home technology for the ultimate in convenience and style.

Upon entry, you are welcomed into a grand living and dining space with high ceilings, perfectly positioned to showcase the breathtaking views. The large, eat-in chef’s kitchen is a culinary masterpiece, outfitted with top-of-the-line stainless steel appliances and custom cabinetry. A spacious laundry room, complete with a washer and dryer, adds an extra layer of practicality to this luxurious home.

The oversized primary suite is a private sanctuary, offering sweeping Central Park views, a serene dressing/seating area, two custom walk-in closets, and a lavish en suite bathroom. This spa-like retreat features a soaking tub, a glass-enclosed shower, and double sinks. Two additional generously sized bedrooms, each with en suite bathrooms and ample closet space, are thoughtfully located in the south wing, ensuring privacy for the primary suite.

Located on Fifth Avenue’s iconic Museum Mile, 1200 Fifth Avenue is an architectural gem designed by the legendary Emory Roth. This pre-war condominium has been meticulously restored and updated, offering residents the perfect blend of historic charm and modern amenities. Just steps from Central Park and the quaint boutiques and eateries of Carnegie Hill, this address epitomizes the best of city living. The building offers a 24-hour doorman, refrigerated storage for deliveries, a bicycle room, and a state-of-the-art gym, ensuring every convenience is at your fingertips.

This residence is not just a home; it’s a statement of sophistication and style, in one of New York City’s most coveted locations.

Welcome to 115 East 87th Street, Residence 37EF, an exceptional cooperative residence located in the heart of Carnegie Hill. Spanning approximately 2,400 square feet, this property offers breathtaking panoramic views of the City skyline, Central Park, the Reservoir, and the East River from a high-floor vantage point.
Thoughtfully designed as a seamless single-level residence, this expansive home offers ten beautifully proportioned rooms, including four generously sized bedrooms and four luxurious marble bathrooms, all impeccably maintained. Bathed in natural light from north, south, and east exposures, the interiors are further enhanced by hardwood floors and an elegant, airy ambiance throughout.
At the center of the home is a sun-filled, windowed chefs eat-in kitchen, outfitted with stainless steel appliances and southern exposure for all-day light. Designed for both everyday living and sophisticated entertaining, the residence features a gracious dining area and an expansive great room that effortlessly accommodates multiple seating and gathering spaces. Additional flexible rooms include a den or home office and a recreation room, offering versatility to suit a variety of lifestyles.
Storage is abundant, with extensive closet space including multiple walk-ins, while a dedicated laundry room with in-unit washer and dryer adds everyday convenience.

Residents of Carnegie Tower enjoy a full-service luxury lifestyle with a full-time doorman, concierge, and resident manager. Amenities include a garage, bike room, additional storage (subject to availability), a beautifully landscaped wraparound terrace on the fifth floor, newly renovated hallways, modernized elevators, a state-of-the-art fitness center, and the building is currently redesigning the lobby and entrance. Smart Latch entry technology provides seamless, phone-based access throughout. All funded by the capital reserve.

Located just off Park Avenue, Carnegie Tower offers unparalleled access to the very best of the Upper East Side. Moments from Central Park and Museum Mile, and surrounded by premier dining, shopping, and gourmet markets such as Eli's, Whole Foods, Fairway, and Butterfield Market, the neighborhood also benefits from convenient transportation options, including the 4/5/6 and Q subway lines, as well as multiple bus routes.
Pied-à-terre ownership, guarantors, and pets are permitted. Up to 75% financing is allowed.
This is a rare opportunity to own a truly exceptional combination apartment in one of Manhattan's most coveted neighborhoods.

Dream sky-high Duplex Penthouse in the building's coveted 'H' line with stunning river views, three bedrooms and three and a half marble bathrooms. Double height 19 feet high ceilings and windows in the living area with a panoramic South and East view of the East River and Roosevelt Island. Oversize master bedroom with two cedar walk-in closets, huge en-suite master bathroom with his and her sinks, huge Jacuzzi tub and separate shower stall. New wide plank wood floors and renovated eat-in kitchen with stainless steel Wolf appliances, Subzero fridge and Bosch dishwasher, Washer/Dryer. The best corner apartment on top of the building with double exposures, the only Penthouse in the building facing South and East with wrap around floor to ceiling windows. It is a luxurious full-service waterfront Condominium with 24 hour concierge, garage, valet, doormen, porters, handymen, live-in superintendent, children's playroom, roof garden, roof top jogging track, roof top business/conference center with wet bar, storage rooms, maid and dry cleaning services, bicycle room and a very big Residents' Lounge on the top floor (with its stunning river views and its own kitchen & bathroom) that is available for residents' private parties. Common charges include your use of the brand new fully renovated State-of-the-art Fitness Centre, Pilates & Yoga Studio, and a gym offering fitness & meditation classes, locker rooms, sauna, steam rooms, massage room, meditation room, cable TV, top floor heated swimming pool and spa tubs with views galore of the East Side of Manhattan. Steps away from John Jay Park.
Superbly located on a quiet cul-de-sac block in the heart of regaled Upper East Side of Manhattan.
Great Location with children’s playground, tennis court, basketball court, health stores, pharmacy, shops, and great restaurants all in close proximity.

Timeless Pre-War Sophistication on the Upper East Side.

Step into this glamorous, sun-filled approximately 3,000 sq. ft. residence, where grand entertaining spaces seamlessly coexist with private, serene quarters. This luxurious four-bedroom plus library home boasts tree-top views, southern exposures, abundant natural light, and the enduring charm of a Classic “9 into 8” pre-war layout.

Off its own semi-private elevator landing, you are welcomed by a 14' x 14' entrance gallery, thoughtfully designed with beautiful symmetry and generous closets—an elegant prelude to the home’s refined spaces. The gallery flows into a palatial double living room, 37' x 20', with five oversized south-facing windows. Perfectly proportioned, the living room is anchored by two striking wood-burning fireplaces on either side, creating a dramatic yet inviting focal point.

The gourmet eat-in kitchen is a chef’s dream, thoughtfully renovated with crisp white custom cabinetry, Caesarstone countertops, rich wood floors, and a cozy banquette. State-of-the-art appliances include a Sub-Zero refrigerator and freezer, Thermador dishwasher, Viking four-burner range and oven. A windowed bathroom completes this bright and cheerful space.

The apartment offers a gracious flow with a split-bedroom layout. The primary suite features a southern exposure, exceptional closets, and a windowed marble bathroom with an oversized shower. On the eastern wing, three additional bedrooms and two full bathrooms provide comfortable, well-proportioned spaces—one with an en-suite bath—perfect for family or guests.
This pre-war home is rich with architectural details, hardwood floors throughout, abundant closets, and an exceptional sense of space.

340 East 72nd Street is an elegant white-glove co-op built in 1930, home to just 33 residences. The building offers a full-time doorman, newly renovated gym, landscaped garden, laundry room, bike storage, and private storage cages. Wood for the fireplaces is provided by the building. Pets are welcome, and the co-op allows 50% financing with a 2% flip tax payable by the buyer.

This extraordinary residence also includes its own staff room on the first floor (maintenance is $598.47), with serene garden views, as well as a massive storage bin.

saleDetailsToCombineWithFederated_data_s:
In move-in, renovated condition, this sun-flooded high floor condo has 4952 SF (~460 SM), 5+ bedrooms and sweeping, panoramic views east and south over the East River as far as the eye can see, and open northern views all the way uptown. Floor-to-ceiling windows, three balconies, brilliant light and views from every single room distinguish this extraordinary duplex.

The elegant gallery hall leads to the living room with space for several seating areas, and a corner dining room that can accommodate 16+ guests. Features throughout include marble, onyx, custom millwork, Lutron lighting, automated shades and extraordinary storage space.

The professional-grade St. Charles eat-in kitchen has custom cabinetry, imported marble countertops and backsplash, a vented Wolf range with griddle, two Sub-Zero fridges, a Miele dishwasher, a Gaggenau steam oven, pot-filler, double sink with disposal, and a pantry. A separate breakfast room includes a built-in banquette. This is a kitchen to love.

The bedroom wing hosts three large bedrooms, each with spectacular views and natural light. The primary suite has river views, a balcony, a sitting area, two custom walk-in closets, one windowed, and a marble bathroom with soaking tub, separate water closet and glass-enclosed shower. There are two separate walk-in closets. The west-facing bedroom has a walk-in closet and en suite bathroom with clawfoot tub and rain shower. 

There are two additional well-sized bedrooms (one used currently as a library/TV room), with substantial closet space and open views. 

In sum, as currently configured, the 22nd floor includes the primary suite, two additional bedrooms in the bedroom wing, a separate guest bedroom with murphy bed and full hall bath access, and a library/TV room that can also function as a bedroom— for up to five bedrooms on the 22nd floor. 

The 21st floor offers a sixth bedroom (now with Murphy bed) with a full bath and functions comfortably as a separate living zone, with media room, kitchen and laundry—the perfect guest quarters, in-law suite, or coveted separate space for the college-aged or adult child. 

Storage is a defining feature, with extensive closet space throughout, including six walk-in closets. 
AMAZING Amenities (all included in the common charges) include a 24-hour doorman, concierge and valet services, live-in super, fitness center with massage room, steam and sauna rooms and fitness classes; a heated rooftop swimming pool, running track, party room, business/conference room, party room with full kitchen and bathroom, children’s playroom, bike room, garage and storage for rent. 

Across from John Jay Park with its outdoor pool, tennis and paddle ball courts, down the block from the Lycée Français, next to The Town School, and around the corner from PS 158. Outstanding nearby dining, shopping, transportation, and neighborhood services. The location lets you get out of town in a snap! 

BEST value on the Upper East Side.

Please note that the current real estate taxes reflect a 17.5% abated amount for use as primary resident. Non-abated real estate taxes are $7,898.82 a month.

Assessment $1,103.88 ends December 2026

Old world elegance and stunning contemporary luxuries abounds in this gracious and expansive home situated in the highly sought-after neighborhood of Carnegie Hill. Located on Park Avenue, a beloved boulevard on the Upper East Side's Gold Coast, this beautiful three-bedroom, three full bathroom home blends original details and scale with modern comforts and luxury amenities.

This sun-drenched 7-into 6-room corner home offers three exposures showcasing not only wide-open city and skyline views from every window but overlooks Central Park treetops and the Reservoir as well. Oversized picture windows frame north, south and west exposures drawing in fabulous natural light throughout the day and presents gorgeous sunset glow at days end. The primary suite features two terraces and a solarium, providing wonderful places to take in the city from all directions.

Contemporary comforts include central air conditioning, fully renovated eat-in kitchen with top-of-the-line appliances, laundry, city-quiet picture windows, custom cove lighting, built-in audio speaker system, and an extraordinary amount of closet storage. Elegant old-world original details including high ceilings, herringbone hardwood floors, a wood-burning fireplace with modern copper mantle, pristine crown and baseboard moldings and an extended 20" formal gallery that offers a distinct elegance.

The gracious layout features nearly 60 feet of formal entertaining space with an elegant living room with gorgeous custom French doors, terrace access, and stunning south-facing open views clear to midtown. The extensive entry gallery leads to the formal dining room that is spacious enough to accommodate entertaining and seating on a large-scale and can even double as a den/media room, if desired.

The stunning eat-in chef's kitchen was completely renovated and beautifully appointed with marble tile floors, granite counters, custom stainless backsplash, and abundant custom cabinet and pantry storage. To the delight of any level cook, top-of-the-line stainless appliances include Sub Zero refrigerator and wine refrigerator, Viking 6-burner with griddle and grill gas range oven with professional vented hood, and Bosch dishwasher. A built-in breakfast banquette completes the space.

The corner primary suite is simply sublime with both western and southern exposures, numerous oversized windows, two planting terraces and a solarium that is great for a sun-filled home office or studio. The suite is extremely private and quiet from the rest of the home and includes a massive custom walk-in closet/dressing room and spacious en-suite bath with original deep-soaking tub and pedestal sink.

Two additional bedrooms are spacious and sun-filled with built-in storage. One features a very large original en-suite bath with both tub and shower, and the other is a corner space with three windows and two exposures. Just outside the third bedroom is a renovated bath with spa like shower, custom millwork, and mosaic tiling, as well as a laundry closet with Electrolux washer and dryer.

1220 Park Avenue is a prestigious, full-service prewar cooperative built in 1930 by one of the most esteemed architects in Manhattan, Rosario Candela. The white glove building's outstanding staff includes a 24-hour doorman and live-in resident manager. Amenities include a state-of-the-art gym, laundry room, bicycle room, and resident storage. Pets are permitted with board approval. Pieds-a-terre are not permitted.

The epitome of full-service, white-glove living on the Upper East Side, Residence 25N at Bristol Plaza is a truly one-of-a-kind, high-floor home offering extraordinary light, scale, and views. Completely reimagined by acclaimed AD100 designer Stephen Shadley, this triple-exposure, three-bedroom, three-and-a-half-bath residence is unlike any other within the condominium.

Spanning approximately 2,500 square feet and featuring 23 oversized windows, the home enjoys sweeping city and Central Park views from nearly every room. A major redesign of the original floorplan creates a seamless flow ideal for both everyday living and elevated entertaining.

A gracious entry foyer welcomes you with sweeping northern views and is outfitted with five custom floor-to-ceiling closets, offering exceptional storage from the moment you arrive. The adjacent dining area leads into the expansive living room, which spans over 40 feet in width and is anchored by a striking circular, floor-to-ceiling windowed sitting area with direct Central Park views — a dramatic and rarely found architectural feature.
The updated kitchen is outfitted with stainless steel appliances, flamed granite countertops, tiled backsplash, and solid wood cabinetry, thoughtfully designed to serve both function and style. Dark-stained hardwood floors throughout the residence add warmth and sophistication.

Just off the foyer is a beautifully tiled powder room with custom vanity and under-counter storage. The king-sized primary suite is a serene retreat featuring an east-facing floor-to-ceiling sitting nook, a walk-in closet, and a windowed en-suite bath with soaking tub and separate shower. Each bedroom is equipped with custom floor-to-ceiling storage and motorized shades for a clean, tailored aesthetic.

The second bedroom enjoys open city and Central Park views through a wall of west-facing windows and features a fully outfitted closet and en-suite bath with soaking tub. The third bedroom, measuring 16' x 11', also includes an en-suite bath and functions equally well as a bedroom or a fully equipped home office.

Bristol Plaza is a 50-story luxury condominium offering white-glove service with a full-time doorman and concierge. Amenities include a residents-only health club with a 50-foot glass-enclosed swimming pool surrounded by sun terraces; a resident lounge; private circular driveway; and on-site garage parking. Ideally located near the 4/5/6, N/W/R, Q, E, and M subway lines, the building offers unmatched convenience in the heart of the Upper East Side.

2% Flip Tax Payable by Purchaser.

This expansive sun-filled corner residence offers a high-floor vantage point, a desirable split-bedroom layout, and generous proportions for comfortable living and entertaining. Enter through a semi-private hallway into a foyer that leads to a great room with northern, eastern, and western exposures, offering open city vistas and Central Park views.

The windowed East-facing kitchen is equipped with top-of-the-line appliances, ample counter and cabinet space, and opens to a generous dining area. Brilliant eastern light and sweeping skyline views create an inviting setting for gatherings.

The home features two primary suites, each appointed with large closets and en-suite bathrooms. Two additional bedrooms offer substantial closet space and enjoy access to two well-sized secondary bathrooms. A secondary gallery leads to a windowed laundry room with a sink, as well as a pantry equipped with a Sub-Zero refrigerator and
wine fridge.

Additional highlights include beamed ceilings, through-wall air conditioning, hardwood floors throughout, and abundant custom-fitted closets. This home is one of the building's most impressive layouts, offering both style and functionality.

875 Fifth is a cooperative built in 1940 and designed by the renowned architect Emery Roth. Located on Fifth Avenue between 68th and 69th streets, the building offers convenient access to Museum Mile, excellent restaurants and shops, and Central Park via the park entrance on 69th Street. Easy transportation includes the Fifth Avenue bus line, the Eastside subways 4, 5, and 6. Amenities include a state-of-the-art windowed fitness center, a central laundry room, a resident lounge and business center, a private garden, bike storage, and private storage. 50% financing allowed, pied-a-terres permitted, 4% flip tax paid by purchaser. An assessment of $825 a month for Capital Improvements through Dec. 2026.

No pets, except service and emotional support animals.
BROWN HARRIS STEVENS RESIDENTIAL SALES LLC
445 Park Avenue 11th Fl, New York, NY 10022
